editionData.title: Daily Brief for May 17, 2026: Global Headlines, Markets, Hacker News, Product Hunt
editionData.description: A daily brief for May 17, 2026 covering global headlines, market closes, Hacker News, and Product Hunt.
editionData.author: Sony Mathew

headlines.title: Venezuela Extradites Billionaire Tycoon Alex Saab to U.S.
headlines.summary: The extradition of Alex Saab, who is tied to a huge graft scheme, is part of a purge of powerful figures who helped the deposed president, Nicolás Maduro, stay in power.

headlines.title: How a Nature Cruise Turned Into a Nightmare
headlines.summary: The hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius set off alarms for a world still traumatized by Covid. For those on board, the danger was much closer.

headlines.title: Fears Grow That Iran May Be Using Proxy Groups Beyond Mideast
headlines.summary: The charges against Mohammad al-Saadi in the United States have raised concerns that Iran could be working with its proxies to stage attacks outside the region.

headlines.title: Car Plows Into Pedestrians in Northern Italian Town, Injuring at Least 8
headlines.summary: Four of the injured were in serious condition, the mayor of Modena said, praising residents who rushed to stop the car’s driver from escaping on foot.

headlines.title: Rival Protests Take Place in London, With a Major Security Effort
headlines.summary: The police have deployed thousands of officers, partly to keep far-right and pro-Palestinian marchers apart. The events drew tens of thousands to London.

headlines.title: Iran plans Hormuz tolls; Trump warns of ‘very bad time’ over stalled talks
Two presidential campaign staffers killed in Colombia as elections near
Poll shows Lula and Bolsonaro tied before Brazil’s presidential election
Eurovision final marred by protests as five countries boycott over Israel
In response to Trump, Taiwan says it is ‘sovereign and independent’
Louisiana Sen. Bill Cassidy loses in Republican primary, does not advance to runoff
headlines.summary: Sen. Bill Cassidy is one of few remaining Republican senators who voted for President Trump's impeachment after the Jan. 6 attack on the Capitol. Trump endorsed his opponent, Rep. Julia Letlow.

headlines.title: Napoleon Solo holds off Iron Honor to win the Preakness
headlines.summary: It's the colt's first victory of the year. Kentucky Derby winner Golden Tempo's exit from the race left the field of 14 horses wide open to contest the middle jewel of the Triple Crown.

headlines.title: Bulgarian banger 'Bangaranga' bags country its 1st Eurovision win
headlines.summary: Bulgaria was not among the favorites to win. But performer Dara's catchy, banging anthem bested 24 other nations at the 70th edition of the international singing extravaganza.

headlines.title: A new Ebola outbreak has already killed 87 people in Democratic Republic of Congo
headlines.summary: Health officials are working to contain a new Ebola outbreak in the DRC of a virus strain with no known vaccine.

headlines.title: North America poised for light display of aurora borealis
headlines.summary: The splashy nighttime phenomenon will be best observed on Saturday and Sunday nights

headlines.title: In step, a partnership that delivers
headlines.summary: When Portugal's Prime Minister Luis Montenegro became in September the first Portuguese head of government to visit China in nearly a decade, one remark he made in meetings with Chinese leaders cut through the familiar tone of diplomatic exchanges.

headlines.title: UAE rejects Israeli visit claim
headlines.summary: The United Arab Emirates has flatly rejected an Israeli claim of a "secret visit" by Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu during the war with Iran, labeling it as "baseless".

headlines.title: Power outage hits eastern half of Cuba
headlines.summary: HAVANA - A power outage knocked out electricity across several provinces in eastern Cuba early Thursday, from central Ciego de Avila to eastern Guantanamo, according to the state-run National Electric Union (UNE).

headlines.title: Nations' friendship still bouncing along
headlines.summary: Deliah Autry, 30, is a member of the United States Women's National Flag Football Team, a three-time world champion, and a doctor of physical therapy specializing in pediatrics.

headlines.title: WWII legacy honors peace and friendship
headlines.summary: While the name Auschwitz echoes through history as a grim symbol of Nazi atrocities during World War II, another camp, half a world away in China's Shandong province, holds a story less known.

headlines.title: PM Modi in Netherlands LIVE: India, Netherlands elevating ties to strategic partnership, says Modi
headlines.summary: PM Modi Netherlands Visit highlights: Follow the latest updates of Narendra Modi's second leg of his five-nation tour, on May 16, 2026

headlines.title: First-ever consignment of Captagon drug worth ₹182 crore seized: Amit Shah
headlines.summary: Amit Shah announces the seizure of ₹182 crore Captagon consignment, reinforcing the Modi government's commitment to a "Drug-Free India."

headlines.title: India rejects Court of Arbitration’s pondage award on Indus Waters Treaty
headlines.summary: India rejects the Court of Arbitration's pondage award on the Indus Waters Treaty, asserting its decision remains in force.

headlines.title: Chola-era Anaimangalam Plates, in possession of Leiden University since 1862, returned to India
headlines.summary: Chola-era Anaimangalam Plates returned from Leiden University to India, marking a significant cultural and historical milestone.

headlines.title: NEET paper leak row: Rahul Gandhi demands Dharmendra Pradhan’s removal
headlines.summary: Rahul Gandhi demands Prime Minister Modi sack Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over NEET paper leak allegations and accountability issues.
